title: Vice President Manufacturing Transformation - Neue Klasse
location: Munich, Germany / Frequent travel to Debrecen and EMEA sites
role_summary:
BMW Group is seeking a senior executive to lead manufacturing transformation activities associated with Neue Klasse and related next-generation EV industrialization programs. The Vice President Manufacturing Transformation - Neue Klasse will be responsible for accelerating industrial readiness, strengthening launch governance, and ensuring that the production system evolves fast enough to support BMW Group's strategic ambitions in electrification, digital manufacturing, and operational flexibility. The role will sit at the intersection of Production, Industrial Engineering, Digitalization, Quality, Purchasing, HR Manufacturing, and Finance.

The successful candidate will drive transformation across existing and new production environments, with particular focus on launch cadence, concurrent engineering, process standardization, digital control towers, and operational governance. This is not a pure strategy role; it requires an executive capable of converting program ambition into plant-level execution. The role must challenge legacy sequencing where necessary while maintaining BMW Group expectations for quality discipline, premium manufacturing standards, and controlled industrial risk.

The position will also play a central role in preparing the organization for higher launch complexity, compressed development-to-SOP cycles, and broader adoption of Industry 4.0 tooling across the production network. Strong change leadership is essential, as the role requires influencing senior plant leaders, aligning with works council and Mitbestimmung realities, and building credibility with stakeholders who may support the strategic direction but resist the execution implications. The ideal candidate combines deep manufacturing credibility with transformation speed, digital fluency, and the ability to lead high-pressure cross-functional programs with executive visibility.
key_responsibilities: Lead manufacturing transformation workstreams linked to Neue Klasse and next-generation EV industrialization.
Drive accelerated launch governance and SOP readiness across new and existing production environments.
Challenge and redesign legacy manufacturing processes where necessary to improve speed, transparency, and execution quality.
Establish cross-functional governance across Production, Quality, Industrial Engineering, Digitalization, Purchasing, HR, and Finance.
Improve manufacturing readiness through digital visibility, performance management, and structured escalation mechanisms.
Lead implementation of Industry 4.0 and smart factory capabilities where they support industrial performance and launch stability.
Support Debrecen and other strategic sites in risk identification, bottleneck removal, and launch recovery planning.
Ensure transformation programs remain aligned with BMW Group quality expectations, BPS principles, and premium brand requirements.
Shape workforce and leadership readiness for new manufacturing models in coordination with HR Manufacturing and plant leadership.
Translate transformation priorities into measurable delivery milestones, resource plans, and business cases.
Represent manufacturing transformation topics in executive steering committees and board-level governance.
Ensure appropriate engagement with works councils and employee representatives when transformation initiatives affect roles, processes, or plant structures.
required_qualifications: University degree in engineering, industrial engineering, manufacturing, operations, or a related technical field.
Extensive senior leadership experience in automotive manufacturing, industrialization, or production transformation.
Demonstrated success in launch, ramp-up, or major transformation programs in complex production environments.
Strong understanding of SOP governance, industrialization risk management, and plant-level execution dynamics.
Experience in digital manufacturing, MES, data-enabled performance management, or Industry 4.0 implementation.
Strong cross-functional leadership capability across Production, Engineering, Quality, Supply Chain, Finance, and HR.
Proven ability to lead change across matrixed organizations with executive-level visibility and pressure.
Sound knowledge of production systems, lean operations, and structured continuous improvement frameworks.
Strong business and financial acumen, including resource prioritization and transformation ROI evaluation.
Fluent English and strong German for executive communication and works council engagement.
preferred_qualifications: Direct experience with EV platform industrialization or battery and e-drive production systems.
Track record in greenfield or major brownfield manufacturing transformation.
Experience in premium automotive manufacturing environments.
Exposure to co-determined industrial environments and German labor relations.
MBA, doctorate, or executive education in manufacturing strategy, digital operations, or organizational leadership.
International leadership experience across Europe or the EMEA region.
leadership_competencies: Leads transformation with urgency while preserving premium manufacturing discipline.
Builds alignment across senior stakeholders with differing incentives, priorities, and risk tolerances.
Creates transparency, ownership, and execution speed in high-complexity industrial programs.
Combines strategic thinking with practical plant-level judgment and operational credibility.
Champions digital innovation only where it creates measurable business and operational value.
Navigates organizational resistance with resilience, clarity, and respect for BMW Group context.

scope_and_scale.total_workforce: Indirect influence across 10,000+ employees through plant and functional leadership
scope_and_scale.annual_budget: EUR 500M-1.0B program and transformation investment influence
scope_and_scale.plants_overseen: Strategic influence across Debrecen and multiple EV-related production sites
scope_and_scale.key_programs: Neue Klasse industrialization, launch acceleration, digital manufacturing enablement, operating model redesign, workforce transformation

context_notes: The role is being created due to increased strategic pressure to accelerate Neue Klasse readiness while maintaining BMW Group standards for launch quality, industrial robustness, and premium execution. Immediate priorities include removing bottlenecks in launch governance, improving digital and operational transparency, and strengthening the organization's ability to execute transformation at the pace required by the market.
